item.title: Reminder: Sonic Racing: CrossWorlds Angry Birds 'Red' Free DLC Racer Out Now
item.content:encoded:
More DLC racers are on the way

Sonic Racing: CrossWorlds has been releasing regular free and paid DLC updates since it launched, and with this in mind, today's update adds Red from Angry Birds to the game

Red will come with his own vehicle, the "Super Roaster", special emotes and sounds, including three new songs for the Jukebox (Go! Theme, Go, Theme! - Final Lap and Angry Birds Theme). Along with this, there'll also be a new festival taking place where racers can earn decals, gadgets and more. Here's a look:

Once again, if you already own this title on Switch or Switch 2, this latest DLC is a free content drop. You can find out what else is on the way to Sonic Racing in our previous post here on Nintendo Life.

Will you be racing as Red in Sonic Racing? Let us know in the comments.

Liam is a news writer and reviewer on Nintendo Life. He's been writing about games for more than 15 years and is a lifelong fan of many iconic video game characters.

item.title: When Will Fortnite Servers Be Back Up? Fortnite April 16 Downtime, Explained
item.content:encoded:
Fortnite is still in the midst of Chapter 7, Season 2, but major changes are coming to various game modes in the April 16 update, which has left Fortnite servers unavailable for a period of time.

Epic Games deploys Fortnite updates every two weeks on average, and, following on from the April Fool’s update, a fresh version of the game is being deployed today.

If you’re itching for your Fortnite fix and want to know when the servers will be available again, Insider Gaming has all the details.

When Will Fortnite Servers Be Back Up?

Fortnite servers are down for several hours for the April 16 update, although the exact time they will return has not been confirmed by Epic Games.

Downtime on April 16, which marks the start of a new Fortnite Festival Season and marks the end of Fortnite Ballistic and Festival Battle Stage, begins at 2 AM ET. While the exact length of downtime has not been shared, previous experience provides an expected time frame.

  • More Insider Gaming: Stone Cold Steve Austin Comes to Fortnite This Week

For mid-season updates, downtime is not as long as the seasonal transitions, like the move to a new Chapter, and, instead, downtime lasts for around three to four hours on average.

Using that metric, the Fortnite servers should be back up around 4 AM to 5 AM ET on April 16, although this is subject to change, particularly if any issues are discovered while the patch is deployed.

What’s In The Fortnite April 16 Update?

The headline changes in the Fortnite April 16 update is the start of a new Fortnite Festival season, alongside the permanent removal of Festival Battle Stage and Fortnite Ballistic.

The removal of Battle Stage and Ballistic was announced last month, alongside Rocket Racing, although the latter has been given a reprieve and will be available to play for significantly longer.

  • More Insider Gaming: Sony Sued Over ‘Bye Bye Bye’ Dance in Deapool & Fortnite

Meanwhile, Laufey is the headline act for the new Fortnite Festival season, while the standard Battle Royale will see the second act of Showdown, including a new Infinity Blade.

Additionally, a new Karaoke Festival mode is landing, alongside Save The World’s move to free-to-play, and new collabs will arrive for WWE superstars Liv Morgan and Stone Cold Steve Austin.

What are you most excited to see in the latest Fortnite update? Share your thoughts in the Insider Gaming Discord.

For more Fortnite, read about seven new game modes leaking, and take a look at when changes to Fortnite Crew come into effect.

MOBILE APP

Level Up Your Gaming News

Real-time news, exclusive podcasts, push alerts, and a better reading experience.

Available on iOS & Android

item.title: eBaseball: PRO SPIRIT 2026 announced for PS5, PC
item.content:encoded:
• PC
  • PS5

Professional Baseball Spirits goes global.

Konami has announced eBaseball: PRO SPIRIT 2026, or Professional Baseball Spirits 2026, for PlayStation 5 and PC (Steam). It will launch digitally on July 16 worldwide for $59.99 / £39.99 / €49.99 / 8,778 yen. Pre-orders are available now. A standard physical edition for PlayStation 5 and 10,978 yen Digital Deluxe Edition will also be available in Japan.

Here is an overview of the game, via its official website:

About

The latest entry in the Professional Baseball Spirits series, eBaseball: PRO SPIRIT 2026 has arrived.

Experience realistic baseball powered by the eBaseball Engine. Every movement and sound inside the stadium is brought to life with remarkable realism. From the flight of the ball and the players’ intense actions to the eruption of the crowd, every moment is recreated with impact. Combined with smooth, live-style play-by-play and commentary (unavailable in English), the game brings you closer than ever to “real baseball.”

Variety of Game Modes

  • 2026 World Baseball Classic (only available in Japan and the Asia region) – Includes 2026 World Baseball Classic, featuring top players from around the globe. Can you lead your favorite team to the top against the world’s elite? Experience the thrill of victory with the team of your choice.
  • Exhibition: Nail‑Biting, High‑Intensity Games – Start by jumping into Versus Mode to fully experience the realistic baseball powered by the eBaseball Engine. Set your stadium, rules, and more, then enjoy an intense matchup with your favorite team.
  • Season: Build a Winning Team – Manage personnel and make strategic decisions with future roster turnover in mind to build a consistently winning team, now with the Active Player Draft included.
  • Star Player: Aim to Become a Star – Use the detailed player customization system to fine‑tune your player, and live out up to 30 years of a pro baseball career with a player built to your liking. Devote yourself entirely to baseball or enrich your personal life as you aim to become a star of the baseball world.
  • Hakkyu No Kiseki: Coach a High School Team to a National Title – Train your players through daily practice and in‑game tactical decisions as you experience a new era of high school baseball with the DH rule now in place. With new first‑years joining and third‑years graduating each season, build your program into a powerhouse capable of reaching Koshien and winning it all.
  • eBaseball: PRO SPIRIT – Compete online against players from around the world. Show your skills, build your very own roster, and fight for the top of the rankings.
  • ❮
  • ❯

2026 Season: Featuring the Latest Data From the 2026 NPB Season

Rosters include new rookies, international players, and returnees to NPB.

More game mode content than any previous title in the series! Includes an updated Hakkyu no Kiseki Mode with the first-ever implementation of the DH rule in high-school baseball, where the next generation of ballplayers vie to become national champions! Also features the introduction of the Active Draft into Season Mode and much more!

(Free updates scheduled to ensure the latest data is available.)

Detail: Textures You Can Practically Feel

The eBaseball Engine replicates all of the sights, sounds and action of the ballpark. The crack of the bat and the flight of the ball, the athleticism of the players, the roar of the crowd, and the play-by-play audio and color commentary are all there!

The eBaseball Engine raises baseball video games to a new level. The game’s next-gen visuals are supported by the Unreal Engine. With overwhelming visual detail, the players, field, and stadium are brought vividly to life.

Play-by-Play and Color Commentary

Japanese language play-by-play is provided by Pro Spirits series regular, Taisuke Mitsuhashi.Color commentary in Japanese is provided by four former greats of the Japanese game: Kimiyasu Kudo, Kenshin Kawakami, Norihiro Akahoshi, and Tomoya Satozaki. And now, for the first time in Pro Spirits history, English-language commentary is also provided by Josh Lewin.

(Color commentary only available when the commentator is set as Taisuke Mitsuhashi.)

World Championship: Real-Time Matchups Against Other Players From Over 200 Countries and Regions Around the World

Pit yourself against other players from around the world in real-time matchups. Create your own team within the cost cap and according to specific tournament rules. Select your team roster from a pool of 120 players. Record a high ranking to receive a rare title.

(Mode included within eBaseball: PRO SPIRIT. Not available in some countries or regions.)

Game Mode: Featuring a Wide Variety of Game Modes

More game mode content than any previous title in the series! Includes 2026 World Baseball Classic Mode (only available in Japan and the Asia region), in which national teams embark on a quest for international supremacy, as well as an updated Hakkyu no Kiseki Mode with the first-ever implementation of the DH rule in high-school baseball, where the next generation of ballplayers vie to become national champions! Also featuring the introduction of the Active Draft into Season Mode, and much more!

Game modes included:

  • Game Modes within eBaseball: PRO SPIRIT 2026
    • Exhibition Game
    • Season Mode
    • Star Player
    • Hakkyu no Kiseki
    • Spirits
    • Live Championship
    • Live Scenario
    • Grand Prix
    • Home Run Derby
    • Team Edit
    • Edit Share
    • Other
  • Game Modes within eBaseball: PRO SPIRIT
    • World Championship

Watch the announcement teaser trailer below. View the first screenshots at the gallery.

Announce Teaser Trailer

Japanese

item.title: Hatsune Miku Logic Paint S+ free update and paid DLC ‘Sakura Miku’ announced
item.content:encoded:
Publisher KOMODO and developer Crypton Future Media have announced a free update and paid downloadable content “Sakura Miku” for Hatsune Miku Logic Paint S+. Both will launch on April 21 for PlayStation 5, PlayStation 4, and PC. A release date for Xbox One and Switch was not announced.

Here are the details:

Premium Downloadable Content: “Sakura Miku” ($4.99)

The premium downloadable content “Sakura Miku” brings a vibrant spring atmosphere to Hatsune Miku Logic Paint S+, featuring a Sakura Miku-exclusive costume for Miku, as well as new cherry-themed costumes, puzzles, and room items. Players can customize their experience with exclusive content and unlock new illustrations as they progress through the game.

  • Sakura Miku Costume for Miku – Equip Miku with the exclusive “Sakura Miku” costume and enjoy a refreshed appearance. The costume is granted immediately upon purchase.
  • New Costumes – Includes a Sakura Miku-exclusive costume for Miku, as well as a new costume that can be worn by all Piapro Characters. All costumes are available immediately upon purchase.
  • Sakura Miku-Themed Room Items – 24 exclusive room items have been added. These items will begin dropping from puzzles after purchasing the premium downloadable content.
  • New Special Puzzles – Additional Special Puzzles are included in the premium downloadable content. Completing them will unlock illustrations that can be displayed in your room.

Free Update

  • New Special Puzzles – New puzzles have been added to the Special Puzzle category. Completing them unlocks illustrations that can be displayed in your room.
  • “Favorites List” for Background Music – A new Favorites List has been added to background music settings for both puzzle gameplay and the main menu, allowing players to play only their selected tracks.

Hatsune Miku Logic Paint S+ is available now for PlayStation 5, PlayStation 4, Xbox One, Switch, and PC via Steam.

item.title: The Prince of Tennis: Sweet School Festival and The Prince of Tennis: Doki Doki Survival remasters launch July 30
item.content:encoded:
In Japan and Asia, the Switch version will also be available physically. Limited edition sets will also be available for 15,400 yen for each game.

The Prince of Tennis: Sweet School Festival 0-40 and more…

■ About

The Prince of Tennis: Sweet School Festival 0-40 and more… is a globally remastered edition of a legendary otome game that has been beloved in Japan for more than 20 years.

■ Key Features

  • Experience a school life filled with romance, surrounded by many charming Princes.
  • Enjoy sweet and bittersweet date events unique to each Prince!
  • Experience elegant, beautifully illustrated event CGs, with some artwork enhanced by animation.

■ Story

During summer vacation, a joint school festival is suddenly announced. This large-scale event is led by the tennis clubs of seven schools selected as representatives in the Kanto Middle School Tennis Tournament. As a second-year middle school student, you are appointed as a member of the festival’s management committee.

Your mission is to help the school you’re assigned to take first place in the stall competition—the highlight of the festival.

Support the players, build trust with them, and make the right calls when it matters. Step by step, work your way toward first place. Your story with your prince begins when you choose which school you’ll represent.

■ Characters

Seishun Gakuen Junior High School

A powerhouse school that won the Kanto Tournament, carrying high expectations for a future national title.

The team includes the mysterious Fuji, the endlessly cheerful Kikumaru, Kawamura—who undergoes a complete transformation the moment he picks up a racket—and the data-driven Inui.

Momoshiro and Kaidoh are complete opposites, creating a tense dynamic between them, while Echizen remains cool and composed no matter the situation.

This colorful group is usually kept in line by the strict Tezuka and the dependable Oishi.

However, during the hectic days of the school festival, there are bound to be moments when their attention cannot reach everyone.

On top of that, Toyama from Shitenhoji Junior High School joins as a special guest—and rumors say he’s quite a handful…

This is a school that demands strong judgment and a deep sense of responsibility, requiring you to step up and make decisive choices when the captain and vice-captain are unable to intervene.

Fudomine Junior High School

With its lineup completely renewed from last year, this up-and-coming dark horse has shaken up tournaments with one surprise after another.

United under Captain Tachibana, the team has overcome significant challenges together, forming strong bonds among its members.

Their connection is so deep that they can come across as somewhat unapproachable, but at heart they are sincere, warm, and deeply loyal.

In addition, Chitose from Shitenhoji Junior High School joins as a special guest, and it is said that he shares a long-standing connection with Tachibana.

If you face them honestly and without hesitation, they are sure to recognize you as one of their own.

St. Rudolph Gakuin Junior High School

A win-at-all-costs team, strengthened by bringing in top students from across the region to support the homegrown core led by Captain Akazawa.

The mastermind Mizuki “writes the scenario” to perfectly control a match in tennis—and that scenario is just as alive in the school festival’s stall competition.

As a member of the festival’s management committee, you will naturally be expected to provide meticulous support that follows his scenario to the letter.

If you can live up to those expectations while also keeping an eye on Captain Akazawa and Yuta—who sometimes struggle to keep pace with Mizuki’s momentum—then you’ll be valued as one of Rudolph.

Yamabuki Junior High School

Not flashy, but steady and reliable—a prestigious tennis school built on solid, unwavering skill.

The team includes Sengoku, the group’s mood maker, Dan, who gives his all no matter the situation, and Shiraishi from Shitenhoji Junior High School, who joins as a special guest and is known for his gentle and considerate nature.

At first glance, Yamabuki seems easygoing and welcoming—but one player demands caution: Akutsu.

Tricked by the tennis club’s advisor, Banda, into taking part in the school festival, Akutsu finds himself here against his will, and his frustration is clear.

Still, he is one of the team. How you choose to interact with Akutsu may become a major turning point during the festival.

Hyotei Gakuen Junior High School

A prestigious school with a brilliant record—but this year, defeat at the Kanto Tournament has only sharpened its hunger for victory.

This is also the school of Atobe, the very sponsor behind the joint school festival. What is required of those serving on Hyotei’s organizing committee is not just high ability.

Above all, it takes the mental strength to withstand the pressure and expectations placed upon them. Having been chosen for a position admired by many, you may find yourself facing sharp words—or even harsher treatment.

Even so, can you stand your ground and keep pushing forward toward victory in the competition? Choosing Hyotei will demand true resolve.

Rokkaku Junior High School

A tennis powerhouse long known as one of Chiba’s veteran contenders. Though every member of the team has known each other since childhood, there is nothing closed-off about their atmosphere.

Raised by the sea and deeply connected to it, they possess hearts as broad and open as the ocean itself. Whether you win the stall competition or not, one thing is certain—you will spend a lively and joyful summer together.

If your goal is to make unforgettable memories at the school festival, Rokkaku Junior High School is the perfect choice!

Rikkai University Junior High School

Proud challengers who uphold the rule of “absolute victory,”never allowing defeat at any time or under any circumstances. That mindset remains unchanged even at the school festival—their sole objective is winning the stall competition.

If you are a perfectionist who resonates with this philosophy, there are few schools that will feel as natural a fit.

In addition, Captain Yukimura, who had been recovering from illness, is set to return for the festival.

By supporting him while he is not yet back at full strength and helping the team work together smoothly, there will be no weaknesses in Rikkai’s path to victory.

The Prince of Tennis: Doki Doki Survival eternal passion! Tie break game

■ About

The Prince of Tennis Doki Doki Survival eternal passion! Tie break game is a globally remastered edition of a legendary otome game that has been beloved in Japan for more than 20 years.

■ Key Features

  • Experience a survival life surrounded by many charming Princes.
  • Enjoy sweet and bittersweet date events unique to each Prince!
  • Experience elegant, beautifully illustrated event CGs, with some artwork enhanced by animation.

■ Story

During summer vacation, an intensive training camp is held for middle school tennis players from across the country. To train their minds and spirits on an uninhabited island, the ship sets sail with the players on board—but they are caught in a storm, and only the players end up washed ashore on the island.

The heroine, whose father is the ship’s captain, and her friend are caught up in the incident as well. With no adults around, they somehow have to survive for the next few days.

The princes seem to be setting up camps in two locations: by the sea and in the mountains. Choose one of the camps, and your life with them begins.

■ Characters

◆ Mountain Route Group

A group based at the mountainside camp, led by Tezuka of Seigaku. While worrying about your father, who went missing during the shipwreck, you continue exploring the island together with dependable players.

What awaits you in the mountain area…?

The Crop Circle (Stream)

While exploring the island, Kikumaru claims to have discovered a crop circle.

With Kamio’s report of a UFO sighting added to the mix, Kikumaru, Kamio, and Mukahi become excited by the idea of an alien encounter—only for Inui to dismiss it outright, stating that the probability is “almost zero.”

Determined to prove Inui wrong, the three decide to search for evidence of a UFO and return to investigate the area upstream where the crop circle was found.

The Mystery Cave and Abandoned Village

Nioh discovers a cave hidden behind a waterfall. Around the abandoned village nearby, Fuji and Yanagi sense a mysterious gaze watching them.

Tezuka dismisses all reports as nothing more than imagination and orders the exploration to be halted.

Feeling something is clearly amiss, Fuji, Tachibana, Oshitari, Sanada, Yanagi, and Nioh defy the order and head toward the abandoned village to investigate further.

The Old Mansion

Kirihara discovers a suspicious old mansion at the edge of a forest that covers much of the mountain area.

After hearing that there are signs of human presence, Ryoma, Kaidoh, Ibu, Hiyoshi, Yagyu, and others take an interest.

However, Tezuka forbids any further investigation, stating that there is no need to examine dangerous places.

Convinced that something is hidden within the mansion, six of them decide to explore it secretly, without Tezuka’s knowledge.

The Ruined Abandoned Hospital

Kenya discovers an abandoned hospital far from camp and proposes investigating the clearly suspicious facility.

Driven by curiosity, Kintaroh and Chitose eagerly agree, while Shiraishi also supports the idea, reasoning that there are no other leads.

Tezuka rejects the proposal, warning that the building contains many dangerous pieces of equipment.

Though the explanation sounds reasonable, the Shitenhoji group cannot shake their sense of unease and secretly heads out to explore the hospital.

Doubts About the Leader

Oishi reports that he has found Sumire Ryuzaki on the island, who had gone missing after the shipwreck.

However, Tezuka not only postpones the suggestion to search for her immediately, but also orders Oishi to remain confined to the lodge for “losing his composure.”

As doubts toward Tezuka grow within the mountain group due to this harsh decision, Oishi, Kawamura, Inui, and Momoshiro appear convinced that Tezuka is acting with a hidden intention.

◆ Sea Route Group

A group based at the seaside camp, led by Atobe of Hyotei.

Using your natural cheerfulness and courage, you build trust with the players while continuing to explore the island

The Crop Circle (Field)

While exploring the island, Minami reports finding a strange pattern resembling a crop circle in an open field.

Jackal’s testimony that he witnessed a UFO-like object fuels the speculation, leading Kentaroh to propose a theory that there may be a UFO base on the island.

While most dismiss it as nonsense, they recruit Yuta—the only one who shows interest—and the four perform a ritual known as “Contactee” in an attempt to summon a UFO.

The Treasure Map

Akazawa discovers a mysterious cave inside a cliff that collapsed during an earthquake.

The location matches a spot marked with a red symbol on an old map found by Shishido.

Excited by the possibility, Kurobane and Marui speculate that it may be a treasure map.

Under Mizuki’s direction, the group examines the map, revealing a hidden message through a heat-based trick and uncovering a map of the cave’s interior.

The Dinosaur’s Shadow

In a fog-covered forest, Sengoku encounters a massive shadow resembling a dinosaur.

Yukimura also claims to have found dinosaur footprints similar to those he has seen in an encyclopedia.

Although Atobe dismisses the idea as ridiculous, the fact that there are two witnesses leaves Sengoku and Yukimura uneasy.

You head toward a nearby lake where the footprints were found along with the eager Akutagawa, Saeki, and Amane who call it a major discovery—and without Atobe’s permission.

Conflict Among the Members

Refusing to interact with the others, Kite, Hirakoba, and Kai isolate themselves at a separate lodge and act independently.

They conclude that the shipwreck was a conspiracy orchestrated by Atobe—and by extension, Hyotei—and continue to treat him as an enemy.

During this time, Atobe is bitten by a venomous snake and falls into critical condition, leading Yukimura to assume the role of acting leader.

Taking advantage of the situation, three members from Higa openly voice their dissatisfaction with Atobe and clash with Otori, who admires him.

At the same time, tensions also rise between Kite and Akutsu.

Watch a new set of trailers below.

item.title: Nintendo Music Celebrates Tomodachi Life Switch Launch With Special Update
item.content:encoded:
Just don't expect any tracks next week

To celebrate the release of Tomodachi Life: Living the Dream for the Switch this week, Nintendo has updated its music app with a special release.

This update for the Switch Online Nintendo Music service on mobile devices adds ten tracks from the new title. Nintendo has also issued a notice mentioning how no new tracks will be released next week:

"Ten tracks from Tomodachi Life: Living the Dream have been added as a special release. Note: Next week there will be no new tracks."

Here are the ten tracks (with a total runtime of 22 minutes) in this latest update:

Tomodachi Life: Living the Dream (Switch) - 16th April 2026

  1. Afternoon on the Island
  2. Mii Creation
  3. Food Mart
  4. Becoming Friends?
  5. Excited 1
  6. Minigames
  7. Gifts
  8. Everyday Life 3
  9. Heart Aflutter 1
  10. Mii News: News Flash

Earlier this week, Nintendo celebrated the Animal Crossing 25th anniversary with the release of the GameCube soundtrack.

Apart from this music update for Tomodachi Life today, Nintendo has also released a new batch of Tomodachi Life: Living the Dream Switch Online icons.

Will you be checking out these tracks on the music app? Let us know in the comments.

Liam is a news writer and reviewer on Nintendo Life. He's been writing about games for more than 15 years and is a lifelong fan of many iconic video game characters.

item.title: Replaced Review
item.content:encoded:
Between the ubiquitous virtual assistants cheerfully patronising us from almost every electronic device and the disposable slop that nobody asked for clogging up our social media feeds, it’s becoming pretty hard to escape artificial intelligence these days – but it could be worse: what if you had an AI actually implanted in your brain? Such is the premise of Replaced, a 2.5D action platformer that follows a scientist named Doctor Warren Marsh who’s on the run after a sentient AI called REACH is shoehorned into his skull. The twist here is that you don’t actually play as the scientist, you play as the AI that’s effectively puppeteering him – with your sole objective to return to the laboratory so you can unpair from your human host. It makes for a compelling 11-hour quest that winds its way through an alternate ‘80s America rendered in a pristine pixel-art style, and one that I ultimately remained engrossed in despite some dull sidequest design and combat that occasionally felt more unresponsive than a muted Siri.

Replaced Review Screens

It really can’t be overstated just how stunning Replaced’s 16-bit inspired game world is. It basically updates the look and feel of classic 2D adventures like the original Prince of Persia and Flashback in a similar way that Octopath Traveler’s HD-2D style transformed SNES-era RPGs, enhancing primitive yet personality-packed character sprites and pixelated landscapes with 3D depth and lighting that really pops. From ruined scientific research facilities, to neon-soaked streets, and down into the flare-lit depths of an underground enemy hideout, Replaced’s world is consistently captivating to explore. Developer Sad Cat Studios is clearly – and quite rightfully – proud of its work, since REACH will occasionally pause to marvel at some immaculately crafted cyberpunk skyline that looms in the distant background. I really appreciated these opportunities to stop and pore over every perfectly crafted pixel.

REACH and Clear

REACH isn’t equipped with a particularly extensive arsenal, but its omniscience seems to include the same martial arts info dump that Neo had installed in The Matrix, since it turns Marsh’s pencil-pushing scientist body into a kinetic killing machine. Armed with a gun that transforms into a baton, REACH is able to indulge in a sort of 2.5D tribute to the Batman: Arkham style of fisticuffs anytime it finds itself surrounded by burly police squads or menacing underworld mutants. In addition to delivering simple strings of skull-cracking combos, coloured indicators that appear above each enemy’s head briefly telegraph when to dodge and when to counter, so Replaced’s skirmishes demand something more than merely button-mashing your baton-smashing. Meanwhile, the use of REACH’s gun is sporadic since it can only be fired after first charging up with successive melee strikes, while getting hit or performing dodges depletes that gauge, putting further emphasis on striking a careful balance between attack and evasion.

It really can’t be overstated just how stunning Replaced’s 16-bit inspired game world is.“

It’s a mostly engaging system, and it gradually introduces welcome strategic wrinkles such as tank-like foes that need their armour stripped off before you can damage them, and other more nimble nasties that will dodge every incoming attack other than counters. It’s just a shame that it’s all regularly undermined by controls that frequently fail to respond – at least in the PC version, even after a day one patch. In particular, the button to apply a medkit routinely failed and often saw me copping a death blow while REACH was seemingly fumbling with its coatpocket. At other times, the input to deflect an enemy gunshot was apparently ignored and REACH’s arms would remain rooted to its sides while it copped a dose of hot lead to the face, which was equally frustrating.

As much as I think Replaced’s visual design is an absolute standout, it too managed to spoil the combat on occasion. Since you’re only ever pit against a set number of enemies at a time, reinforcements often linger in the background waiting for their turn to enter the fray like the curiously courteous extras in a Bruce Lee movie. The trouble is that it’s extremely tough to discern one 2D plane from the other in the heat of battle, and I’d invariably find myself trying to land blows on enemies that technically weren’t there, making me swish my baton through an empty space and leaving myself open to a genuine threat waiting to strike while I was distracted.

Thankfully, I found Replaced’s platforming sections far more consistent. Though it doesn’t ever reach the heights of Shinobi: Art of Vengeance or Ninja Gaiden: Ragebound’s complex midair maneuvering, it is fun to wall-jump your way up narrow elevator shafts, monkey-swing along suspended ceiling pipes, and carefully flip past electrified surfaces. There are also a number of interesting environmental puzzles to solve throughout, such as carefully positioning industrial fans to boost the double-jumps powered by REACH’s multipurpose pistol. To be fair, there are some cases where the checkpointing seems a little severe, and a number of times I had to painfully retrace lengthy platforming stretches after mistiming a jump and falling to my doom, but for the most part I got a kick out of Replaced’s pixelated parkour.

Attack of the Drones

When you’re not throwing down with increasingly well-armed riot squads or fighting a one-on-one battle with gravity as you scramble and flip up the side of a skyscraper, Replaced changes things up in the form of regular instant-fail stealth sections. Initially these deadly encounters with surveillance drones feel pretty uninspired, and patiently waiting for the arc of a spotlight to swing away from your position so you can scramble to safety behind a stack of conveniently placed crates is something that’s been repeated in the likes of Limbo and the Little Nightmares series many times over at this point. However, Replaced eventually makes its sneaking a little more involved with the welcome addition of a hacking system, which both introduces a neat little shape-matching minigame, as well as the ability to temporarily disable enemy turrets or create distractions for mechanical sentries. I appreciated the more puzzle-oriented form of stealth that Replaced’s hacking provided, I just wish it had been introduced earlier on.

Those stealth sequences may demand a rigidly slow and steady approach, but it's the story pacing that staggers to a crawl each time you return to the train station hub between outings. This combat-free zone offers a series of sidequests of the bog standard go-here-and-fetch-that variety, forcing you to shlep your way through the various tent-lined streets and makeshift hospital areas inhabited by homeless hordes in search of trivial items like missing comic books or food for a local dog. While it’s true that much of this busywork is entirely optional, skipping these uninteresting chores means you miss out on vital rewards like health boosts and expanded medkit slots that prove to be pretty crucial against the heavier enemy groups in the story’s second half. I would have preferred if these menial tasks had been scrapped in favour of just buying upgrades with a currency or an XP system before hurrying back to the more exciting main objectives, but as it stands each trip to Replaced’s train station felt like my progress would go off the rails a bit.

That said, I was a big fan of the playable arcade cabinets found in the station’s basement level, as well as the funny interactions with the sassy young girl who operates them. The inclusion of playable Frogger and Space Invaders clones may seem pretty antiquated in an era when each Like a Dragon adventure features fully-working Sega Rally or Virtua Fighter machines, but since they each have a high score table you better believe I still sat on them until I topped it. Those aren’t the only nostalgic nods I appreciated either. I also loved the Wingman, a sort of GameBoy and Walkman hybrid that can be whipped out of REACH’s pocket in order to play catchy collectible synth tunes or to scan the environment for enthralling scraps of story.

Sad Cat Studios has crafted one heck of a dazzling sci-fi dystopia.“

In fact, it’s the story, in tandem with the jawdropping visual design, that ultimately had me hooked on Replaced all the way through to its rousing climax, in spite of any issues I had with its frequently fussy controls or sidequest stumbles. REACH’s journey and evolution feels surprisingly human given its artificial origins, and the quirky cast of side characters show plenty of personality despite the fact that each and every interaction with them is entirely text-based (even the so-called ‘audiologs’ you collect in the environment can only be read rather than heard). Sad Cat Studios has crafted one heck of a dazzling sci-fi dystopia, and with some post-release patching it could still be elevated into something really special.

Other Great Games with AI Characters

See All

Verdict

Its combat may be literally hit and miss at times, its sprinkling of sidequests are almost exclusively ho-hum, and its stealth sections take a little too long to properly get into gear. Yet somehow Replaced remains a consistently absorbing piece of pixel art-powered action platforming that I struggled to put down, from the exciting science lab breakout in its opening moments to the captivating climax some dozen or so hours later. Sad Cat Studios should be commended for crafting such a gripping and gorgeous 2.5D action platformer, even though Replaced is one AI-based adventure that could do with a system update to completely iron out the bugs.



https://www.ign.com/articles/replaced-review
item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot

item.title: Switch 2 Rating For Bethesda's Newest IP Appears Online
item.content:encoded:
Is a port of Starfield on the way?

Bethesda has already shown strong support for the Switch 2 with multiple game releases, but one title that hasn't landed on the hybrid platform is its new space-themed RPG, Starfield.

Following its recent launch on the PlayStation 5 (which admittedly shipped with some issues), a new rating for a Switch 2 version of the game has apparently been discovered on the Taiwan classification board. This rating follows ongoing rumours throughout 2025 about Starfield being released on Nintendo's new platform.

Of course, while ratings can often lead to an announcement, it's doesn't necessarily guarantee a release will go ahead. In this particular case, nothing has been officially announced by Bethesda (or other parties) about a port of this title for Nintendo's new platform.

This follows a rating for a Nintendo version of Diablo 4: Lord of Hatred surfacing on the Indonesian rating board this week.

Bethesda has already released The Elder Scrolls V: Skyrim Anniversary Edition and Fallout 4: Anniversary Edition on the Switch 2. It's also got The Elder Scrolls IV: Oblivion Remastered on the way, and next month is releasing Indiana Jones and the Great Circle on the platform.

Would you be interested in playing Starfield on Nintendo's platform if any announcements follow this rating? Let us know in the comments.

[source universonintendo.com, via videogameschronicle.com]

Liam is a news writer and reviewer on Nintendo Life. He's been writing about games for more than 15 years and is a lifelong fan of many iconic video game characters.

item.title: Sonic Racing: CrossWorlds ‘Angry Birds Content Drop’ update now available
item.content:encoded:
SEGA has released the “Angry Birds Content Drop” update for Sonic Racing: CrossWorlds, the company announced.

The update will include the following content:

  • Red from Angry Birds as a playable character
  • Red’s vehicle, the Super Roaster
  • Special emotes and sounds, including three new songs added to the Jukebox:
    • “Go! Theme”
    • “Go! Theme (Final Lap)”
    • “Angry Birds Theme”

An in-game “Angry Birds Festival” will also run from April 16 at 5:00 p.m. PT / 8:00 p.m. ET until April 19 at 4:59 pm. PT / 7:59 p.m. ET.

Sonic Racing: CrossWorlds is available now for PlayStation 5, Xbox Series, Switch 2, PlayStation 4, Xbox One, Switch, and PC via Steam and Epic Games Store.

item.title: EXCLUSIVE: First Details On Lacrosse 26’s Gameplay And Franchise
item.content:encoded:
For the first time in eight years, lacrosse fans are getting a new video game. Announced on Tuesday night, Lacrosse 26 is a next-gen lacrosse game coming to PlayStation 5 and Xbox Series X|S.

While details have been slim, aside from its platforms and the release date of April 24, Insider Gaming has learned exclusive details on the game’s development and what players can expect.

The game has been in development since 2020 and was built from the ground up in Unity.

“From the start, everything was built from scratch,” Crosse Studios founder Carlo Sunseri told me. “Early prototypes even used empty capsules on the field just to get the AI working.”

Lacrosse 26 was built to be more on the simulation side than arcade. The goal, he said, is to “mimic real lacrosse as closely as possible while keeping it fun for a video game.”

Sunseri said that the team worked with an “experimental motion-matching animation system”. They invested in a motion capture system to track stick rotation for the first time, and also built out features like ball physics, lighting, replay, and more.

Going back to the motion-matching system, Lacrosse 26 features over 200 animations just for player movement. That’s on top of the hundreds of animations for catching, goalie saves, passing, groundballs, and shooting. Compared to games from the past that he’s made, Sunseri says that everything feels “grounded.”

“The biggest difference is how grounded everything feels and how realistically the lacrosse movement is represented,” Sunseri said. “With the new motion capture and motion-matching system, animations aren’t just triggered; they blend dynamically based on speed, acceleration, and player input.”

Franchise Mode Is The Focus

When it launches on April 24, Lacrosse 26 is a field lacrosse game that will offer players a few modes to play, such as quick play, practice, and local multiplayer, but the primary focus is on Franchise.

Franchise mode, which is run more like a college dynasty, starts by having you choose a team name, logo, uniform, names, and player headshots. After you have your team, you’ll play through a full season.

  • More Insider Gaming: GTA Leak Raises Take-Two Interactive Stock Value By Over $1 Billion

As you play through the season, recruits will start showing interest in your program. Using the XP earned while playing, you’ll be able to improve your recruiting efforts towards players and allocate various resources to attract talent.

“At the same time, you can spend money and XP to develop and train your current players, helping them grow throughout their college careers,” Sunseri said. “Each season, your roster evolves naturally, since players graduate after four years and are replaced by incoming recruits.”

Players will also be able to schedule tougher or easier opponents between seasons. Winning the tougher games will help build a reputation, increasing your chances of landing better recruits.

Lastly, you’ll also be able to play through Franchise Mode with up to four friends on the same team.

Are you going to pick up Lacrosse 26 when it launches on PS5 and Xbox Series X|X on April 24? Leave your thoughts in the comments below, and in this official Insider Gaming Discord.

Mike has been covering the gaming industry since 2012, and has reported on some of the largest events in the industry while also working as an investigative reporter. Outside of…

More from Mike Straw

item.title: Walmart Has the Lowest Price on an Nvidia GeForce RTX 5070 12GB Graphics Card in 2026
item.content:encoded:
If you're planning out a PC build and have been hoping to get one of the new Nvidia Blackwell graphics cards at a reasonable price, this might be the best opportunity you'll get in the near future. Walmart is offering a retail boxed PNY GeForce RTX 5070 12GB graphics card for just $599 shipped. Mind you this is still $50 over MSRP, but it's the best price I can find right now for a standalone 5070 GPU and the first time I've seen this card drop below $600 this year.

PNY GeForce RTX 5070 12GB OC Graphics Card for $599

PNY GeForce RTX 5070 12GB OC Graphics Card

The GeForce RTX 5070 GPU is an excellent choice for 1080p or 1440p gaming. Compared to the previous generation GPUs, the RTX 5070 offers a slight performance improvement over the RTX 4070 Super, which was and still is an excellent GPU. The fps gain is greater in games that support DLSS 4.5 with multi-frame generation. Read our RTX 5070 review for our hands-on impressions. This PNY model features a slim 2.4-slot triple-fan cooling system and a slight overclock.

Eric Song is the IGN commerce manager in charge of finding the best gaming and tech deals every day. When Eric isn't hunting for deals for other people at work, he's hunting for deals for himself during his free time.

item.title: Sky: Children of the Light Players Can Build Their Own Challenges And Partake In Festivities In New Season of Carnival
item.content:encoded:
In the new long-term Season in the social adventure MMO Sky: Children of the Light arriving this week, players get to not only help out the Wandering Carnival’s festivities, but also make carnival-like challenges of their own. 

In this “Season of Carnival, players are beckoned to help set up the Wandering Carnival—and of course, when that’s done, they can partake in its activities and reap the rewards as well. 

Five Seasonal Quests will be unlocked throughout the season, on April 17, May 1, May 15, May 29 and June 12. Each brings different challenges to take on, including an obstacle course, some ball shooting, and hanging up flyers for the grand opening of the Carnival. 

Sky players will also get to create and join Shared Space Challenges, which will be available to build and play beyond the season. 

The Seasonal Quests will unlock different components for the Shared Spaces, including some items that’ll help create limitations like time limits for the sake of challenges, as well as obstacles and goals like Bounce Pads and Challenge Tokens. By using a Challenge Flag, players can join these specific player-built challenges. 

Welcome to the Wandering Carnival! ??Friendly faces have returned to put on a show for all Sky Kids, but they need your help to set up! Unleash your creativity as you build and participate in exciting new shared Space Space Challenges ?Are you ready? The carnival awaits ?? pic.twitter.com/4q5srU2qpp

— Sky: Children of the Light (@thatskygame) April 15, 2026

At the end of the season, at an unspecified date, players will be able to “enjoy the carnival alongside lots of other Sky Kids,” likely meaning there’ll likely be one last grand area to enjoy as many Seasons and events have. As usual, there’ll be an array of rewards to both earn and purchase in the shop. 

This new Season of Sky launches this Friday, April 17 and runs until July 2. Already, there’s the Earth Day event going on until April 20, and there’s been a major patch this week that adds some new social capacities and brings some fixes.



https://www.mmorpg.com/news/sky-children-of-the-light-players-can-build-their-own-challenges-and-partake-in-festivities-in-new-season-of-carnival-2000137815

item.title: Titan Quest II's Summons Update is Live, With Reworked Epics, and Mastery Updates
item.content:encoded:
Summons are finally here in Titan Quest II. The fan-favorite feature from the original game has made it into the sequel in a big Early Access update.

Each Mastery gets a summon in today’s patch, which will eventually lead to the team making a full summoner playstyle in the game. According to their plans, this will be possible when the Spirit Mastery is released later this year. Today’s patch adds summons and options on how you might choose to play with them.

If you want a more passive playstyle and let your summons go out there and do the bulk of the fighting for you while you stay and react to  what's going on and issue commands, you can do that. if you want to get right in there and use your summons to fight alongside you, that’s also possible. The options depend on the summon, as sending out a War Banner for War Mastery will play differently than sending out the Core Dweller to pummel your enemies. 

The War Banner, Warfare Mastery’s summon, just stands there, healing and buffing allies that come within its aura, and nerfing enemies. The Core Dweller is a tanky rock golem summon that can do what you’d expect from a golem: slam enemies, casting fissures in your place, and can even be more powerful via channeling. Today’s update also adds the Wisp for Storm Mastery and the Shadow Clone for Rogue Mastery.

Talismans also arrive, a new off-hand choice you can use instead of a shield. These offer a guaranteed increase to energy, plus some buffs and can help a number of different builds,  especially useful for casters but non-casters can make use of them.

This is a pretty big update, and the patch notes go into all of the extensive info you’ll ever need. One of the other big parts of this update is a rework of Epics. Grimlore  has done a pass and buffed almost every one of them. Not only does the team run down on every single change made to Epics, affixes, and item stats, we get info on UI changes, fixes, and more.



https://www.mmorpg.com/news/titan-quest-iis-summons-update-is-live-with-reworked-epics-and-mastery-updates-2000137812

item.title: Dosa Divas Makes Me Hungry
item.content:encoded:
There’s a commercial I see when I watch television shows on streaming services for store-bought naan that drives me fucking crazy. The characters in this commercial repeatedly describe naan as “a delicious fluffy flatbread,” in a way that feels like they want to erase all the connections that naan has to a culture and a place. They don’t mention India, or Pakistan, or any of the Asian and Arab countries where naan is a typical accompaniment to meals. It’s a flatbread! A delicious, fluffy flatbread! In the same way that Taco Bell strives to divorce the food it produces from its cultural context—food that comes from nowhere and has no history—this commercial took a food I have always known as Indian and divorced it from Indianness. Dosa Divas is a game that allows me to release some of the anger I feel about this.

My relationship to the Indian diaspora is complicated. My mother immigrated when she was seven, and her family immigrated before the cap on Asian immigrants was repealed in 1965, meaning that there was no diaspora culture to greet her in America. Add to that that she married a Black man, a lot of Indian Americans just don’t want to have anything to do with me. No joke, I have had many other Indians either tell me I am not Indian or straight up stop speaking to me after learning these facts. 

While there are examples of diaspora media products that speak to me—the 2001 movie American Desi, a shortening of the phrase “American Born Confused Desi,” and the music of Heems and MIA—I feel alienated by stories that describe an immigrant experience I have never known. Food was often the one cultural connection I had to other Indians. My mom still makes the recipes she remembers from her youth, and we both follow the handwritten recipes in a notebook that my ammama left for us. But I also find that stories about food in the diaspora community are often pretty shallow, reducing a rich culture into a small number of foods that come from a very specific area of India, or god forbid, telling yet another story about white kids thinking your lunchbox is stinky. 

Dosa Divas is not a story about the worthiness of Indian food, nor is it about what white people think about Indian food. From what I’ve played so far, it is a story about how food gets commodified, repackaged, and divorced from the things that make cooking and eating an essential part of creating a community.

Dosa Divas follows sisters Amani and Samara as they take a roadtrip through a science fiction world inspired by Indian culture. Their destination is their old family restaurant, but on the way they discover that their other sister Lina has taken the family business and turned it into an evil corporation that sells meals in tubes. If Lina had the idea to repackage naan as delicious fluffy flatbread, I am certain that she would, but her mission goes farther than that—she wants to rid the world of cooking entirely so that everyone has more time to work sixteen hour shifts in the mines.

As Amani and Samara travel to their hometown, they encounter hungry people who desperately want dosa—and dosa they provide. In the first town, a fishing village, dosa is the thing that empowers people to repair bridges and start planning about how to fight off Lina’s corporate goons. Each dosa is carefully crafted by Amani and Samara together, from ingredients either found in the environment or bartered for. The cooking minigames have not outstayed their welcome so far, but more than the minigames, I love the many different recipes for dosa that you collect as the game goes on. You learn how to make spicy fish dosa and sweet banana dosa and savory veggie dosa, all of which can later be used as healing items during turn-based battles.

In combat, where you face off against Lina’s lawyers, each of the characters' skills and attacks are assigned a flavor value—essentially just taking the RPG genre convention of different elements and replacing them with Salty, Sweet, Sour and Savory. But by attacking an enemy and using their flavor weakness against them you can break their shields and make them Stuffed, allowing you to do more damage. So far it’s just complex enough to be fun without feeling unwieldy, but the (ahem) flavor text around these character abilities really makes them come together for me. Uncle Hinti, the disembodied character who delivers on-screen hints, tells the player that Stuffed enemies are too busy dreaming of a home-cooked meal to bother protecting themselves from attacks. Who among us hasn’t drifted into a daydream thinking about Mom’s idli and walked into scaffolding?

More than anything, Dosa Divas makes me ravenous. I wish more diaspora stories were about how our culture can and is commodified, rather than arguing for its worthiness or reinforcing a specific narrative of diaspora that excludes people like me. But it also, specifically, makes me hungry for dosa. My mom told me that ammama made dosa so thin and crispy and perfectly golden brown that she was gifted with a golden spatula—not very useful for cooking, but a compliment to her skill. I can still taste her dosa in my mouth, hot and oily and just a little bit sour. I can’t have those dosa again, but someday I hope to at least follow her recipe.



https://aftermath.site/dosa-divas-outerloop-outersloth-indian-food-capitalism/
item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot

item.title: Grounded 2 Brings A Big Lizard Boss And Buggy Progression In Newest Update
item.content:encoded:
A new major content update has dropped for the early access bug-themed survival game Grounded 2, with a scaly new foe to take on and big Buggy improvements. 

The big highlight for many this patch will be the new boss, King Dozer, who is straight-up a big ol’ lizard (relatively speaking, given the premise of the game). Players are urged to suit up before they summon this boss and endure the onslaught of big physical attacks and magma launched from the lizard’s mouth. The rewards are plentiful as always, including new weapons and armor, gear, and even furniture and trophies. 

Also new to check out is the Sinkhole Anthill, which promises new creatures, gear and resources to find. Plus, there are apparently mutated creatures from the O.G.R.R. labs lurking around. 

Buggies are getting a big improvement with this patch with the new Buggy Progression system, letting players upgrade and customize these mounts. For now, players can use Moldy Milk Molars to improve health, damage and effectiveness; improve support systems; and even bring them into tougher fights. It also seems there’ll be active and passive mutations that’ll help players reinforce the buggy’s specialty. 

Plus, this Grounded update brings the previously-announced Playground custom map mode, as well as other additions and fixes including blocking while holding a bow, Easter objects, inventory navigation improvements and more, as listed in the full patch notes. 



https://www.mmorpg.com/news/grounded-2-brings-a-big-lizard-boss-and-buggy-progression-in-newest-update-2000137814

item.title: Gears of War: E-Day Direct Runtime Revealed by Xbox CCO
item.content:encoded:
The length of the forthcoming Gears of War: E-Day presentation has been revealed.

Gears of War: E-Day is slated for release sometime this year. Currently, no real footage has been shown since 2024. The game was initially announced at the 2024 Xbox Showcase in June; at the time, only a cinematic trailer was shown. of that year.

However, new footage of the game will be shown during the Gears of War: E-Day Direct presentation on June 7. This news was recently confirmed by an Xbox executive.

Gears of War: E-Day Direct Length Revealed Ahead of June Event

Xbox Chief Content Officer Matt Booty recently appeared on the official Xbox podcast and confirmed that the presentation would run about 30 minutes. The Standalone presentation is separate from the Xbox showcase that will be held just prior to the Gears of War content being shown.

“I’ll say that we’ve got the Gears Direct coming, and after seeing that, we’ve probably got like a full 30 minutes, which is really great. And I don’t know. I just love everybody in that studio, the work they’re doing. I’m excited about the game, and we’re going to have a fantastic, full 30 minutes of stuff to see.”

Furthermore, Booty was asked about being promoted to the role of Xbox CCO. He was announced as the CCO in the wake of Phil Spencer’s retirement.

“The title is more than anything a reflection of the work that the studios do. It is so core to who we are as Xbox and all the work that all our teams do across all our studios. It’s a validation of their work,” he said. “The title, you see it in other places. You see it in film, you see it in publishing.

“I think it brings, again, a validation that says that one, Xbox takes these studios seriously, that Microsoft takes it seriously, and that content, things we build; all our great games [are] important, so that’s really what it’s about. For me, my job stays the same.”

Elsewhere in the interview, Booty discussed how many of Microsoft’s game studios work hand in hand on new releases. Read more here.

Are you excited for Gears of War: E-Day and the upcoming presentation? Sound off in our official Discord server. Also, don’t forget to sign up for our weekly newsletter.

item.title: Players Use Epic For Free Games But Then Just Go Back To Steam, Former Employees Claim
item.content:encoded:
The vibe around the Epic Games Store is that people love it for the free stuff but don’t use it for much else. That’s an accurate read according to the Los Angeles Times, which recently outlined a pattern of half-baked plans and rushed releases.

Its report cited two former Epic employees who claimed that players love the Epic Games Store for the free games but then immediately go back to Steam after claiming them. The result is an alternative to Valve’s PC gaming storefront that’s still struggling to meaningfully compete.

The company reported that it had 78 million monthly active users last year and earned $400 million from non-Epic games in 2025. Epic global comms director Liz Markman told the Los Angles Times that those metrics were “lower than our growth expectations at launch.”

The company is now trying to bundle its PC gaming storefront and mobile gaming app into a unified platform across different devices, but the ambition behind that plan has drawn scrutiny from people who claim Epic has a tendency to rush things. According to the Los Angeles Times, the company spent millions on contractors to push out the 2024 mobile game store app in just seven months.

Bloomberg recently reported that Epic is also working on a new extraction shooter with Disney which is part of a $1.5 billion partnership. Will that project be able to channel the success of Fortnite or be another victim of Epic’s many side quests, which have included everything from buying Art Station to buying and later selling Bandcamp?

To the degree that Epic succeeds or fails, it will be ordinary employees who face the consequences. Epic recently laid off over 1,000 staff, including one who was terminally ill with brain cancer, after an unexpected drop in Fortnite engagement last year.



https://kotaku.com/epic-free-games-store-steam-tim-sweeney-2000687958
item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot

item.title: A Switch 2 Version Of Starfield Leaks As The RPG Struggles On PS5
item.content:encoded:
The Switch 2’s first year hasn’t been quite the open season for current-gen ports that some had expected. That’s starting to change, however. A rumored Switch 2 version of Starfield has just been leaked by the Taiwan ratings board a week after Bethesda’s sci-fi epic finally hit PlayStation 5. Will it have more success on Nintendo’s hardware than it’s had so far on Sony’s platform?

As spotted by Universo Nintendo, the RPG was recently rated by the Taiwan Entertainment Software Rating Information Board. Bethesda has yet to officially confirm the game is headed to Nintendo’s newest console, but it’s not surprising given that Indiana Jones and the Great Circle will be arriving there next month. The Microsoft-owned company’s also been busy with ports for Fallout 4 and The Elder Scrolls IV: Oblivion Remastered, which is slated to arrive on Switch 2 sometime later in 2026.

While ports will help Bethesda’s bottom line as it works on The Elder Scrolls 6, the staggered release schedule may have dampened the reception of some of its games on non-Xbox consoles. Data analyst Rhys Elliott at Alinea Analytics recently estimated that Starfield had only sold around 140,000 copies on PS5 in its first week, a drop in the bucket compared to its over 15 million existing players previously reported.

That’s a lot better than Avowed but pails in comparison to ports like Forza Horizon 5. Were PS5 players just not buying the Starfield hype, or is there some loss of novelty as Sony’s platform becomes flush with blockbusters that were previously Xbox exclusives? Or maybe some players are just waiting for all of the crashes to be fixed before hopping into Bethesda’s latest RPG adventure.

We don’t know when Starfield will arrive on Switch 2 but it’s likely to be only one of many new ports coming to the hardware in 2026. The Indonesian ratings board recently leaked that Diablo 4 is also set to make the jump at some point this year.



https://kotaku.com/switch-2-starfield-port-leak-ps5-sales-xbox-2000687942
item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot

item.title: FaZe Clan Announce FaZe Up Next Rainbow Six Academy Team
item.content:encoded:
FaZe Clan has unveiled a new academy roster for Rainbow Six as part of its new academy esports programme.

On April 14, the org unveiled its FaZe Up Next academy initiative but didn’t reveal many details other than promising to usher in a ‘new generation of FaZe Esports.’

Rainbow Six came as a surpirse to some, with angry Counter-Strike fans demanding an update following the shocking departure of IGL karrigan to Team Falcons.

FaZe has what is essentially the Rainbow Six world champions after its all-Brazilian roster won the Six Invitational earlier this year.

This was the second year in a row that FaZe won the Six Invitational and was the third consecutive year they made the final.

Now, to support that roster and the organisation’s future, FaZe has picked up a lot of promising talents from the second tier of Brazilian Rainbow Six Siege esports.

The FaZe Up Next R6 roster is:

  • Hurbyz
  • Hax
  • Felpsz
  • Vinicius ‘Lendao’ Siqueira
  • Jonathas ‘Jow’ Pinto
  • Rules (Coach)

Some Rainbow Six esports fans may recognise the name Jow as he previously played for 9z Team. There, he played against FaZe during the South America League 2025, though his team finished last.

Following the announcement, coach Rules tweeted out about his excitement and thanked members of FaZe:

“Very excited to start this new project and grateful for the opportunity, especially to RafadeLL and Ivan Roichman who did everything to make it happen and bring this idea off the paper! Grateful to represent such a great organization. I’m ready to give my best.”

Do you think FaZe will pick up academy teams in CS and Call of Duty too? Let us know on the Insider Gaming Discord.

Meanwhile, the main FaZe roster will be competing at the upcoming BLAST R6 Major Salt Lake City tournament.

item.title: World of Warcraft Classic Announces MoP Classic Bonus XP and Overlords of Outland for The Burning Crusade Classic Anniversary Edition
item.content:encoded:
Blizzard announced two upcoming dates to be aware of in World of Warcraft Classic. Mists of Pandaria Classic is about to get the Joyous Journeys bonus days next week. Plus, The Burning Crusade Classic Anniversary Edition will see its next big update, Overlords of Outland, in May.

Overlords of Outland will launch globally on May 14th, bringing the fight for Outland to the center of the action. There will be two new raids to challenge, The Eye and Serpentshrine Cavern. There are new reputation factions to get to know, and Arena Season 2 will kick off, and there will also be rewards. 

Originally Posted by Blizzard(Blue Tracker )

The battle for Outland heats up on May 14 as WoW Burning Crusade Classic Anniversary Edition: Overlords of Outland launches globally on all realms at 3:00 PM PDT / 23:00 BST.

Take the fight to Illidan Stormrage’s lieutenants, brave two new raids, and chase new rewards across the shattered world of Outland.

  • Two New Raids: The Eye and Serpentshrine Cavern
  • New Reputation Factions: Ogri’la and Sha’tari Skyguard
  • Arena Season 2
  • New Profession Recipes
  • Druid Swift Flight Form

Get ready to adventure across the shattered skies and torn lands of Outland as you continue to protect Azeroth from the threat of destruction, beginning May 14.

Joyous Journeys offers a solid +50% XP gain up to level 90 for any character. So, if you’ve been looking to roll a new character or just want to catch up a bit, these bonus days are going to help. They begin April 21st and will last until the next content update arrives.

Originally Posted by Blizzard(Blue Tracker)

Stalwart champions in World of Warcraft: Mists of Pandaria Classic, take advantage of the Joyous Journeys experience buff to enhance your odyssey through Azeroth, increasing experience gains by 50% up to level 90!

Duration: April 21 until the next content update for Mists of Pandaria Classic arrives.

The mystical lands of Pandaria are swirling with danger and need your help. Create a new character, finish leveling a forgotten one, or invite friends to join you in legendary Pandaria.

The Joyous Journeys buff is active by default; however, you can visit any innkeeper in the capital cities and Dalaran to turn it on or off. The Joyous Journeys buff is not available in modern World of Warcraft® (currently Midnight), World of Warcraft®: Burning Crusade Classic Anniversary Edition, or World of Warcraft® Classic Era.

See you in Pandaria!



https://www.mmorpg.com/news/world-of-warcraft-classic-announces-mop-classic-bonus-xp-and-overlords-of-outland-for-the-burning-crusade-classic-anniversary-edition-2000137813

item.title: New Steam Feature Could Make It Easier To Avoid Getting Ripped Off
item.content:encoded:
Dataminers have recently found code that points toward Valve adding two nifty features to Steam, its uber-popular digital PC gaming store. One feature will show you the lowest price a game has sold for in the last 30 days. Meanwhile, another feature might tell players how well a game will perform on their PC without them having to buy it and try.

As recently spotted by Valve fansite LambdaGeneration, dataminer SigaTbh used SteamDB to dig around Steam’s innards and found something very interesting. Buried in Steam is new code that references “Price History” and “30-Day Low.” This sounds a lot like Valve is planning to implement a feature in Steam that would indicate a game’s lowest recent price, which might help you avoid buying something that’s not been on sale for a bit or which just got off a big $1 sale.

What would this look like in practice? Well, we don’t have to guess. This 30-day lowest price feature already exists for some Steam users in other parts of the world. For example, this has been a feature in the European Union since June 2023. Valve had to add it to comply with new consumer protection guidelines and rules included in the EU’s Omnibus Directive. So, here’s what the 30-day low price feature will probably look like whenever it goes live in the United States.

Steam now shows 30-day low price in some European Union countries to comply with the Omnibus Directive. pic.twitter.com/BbTsNn2Zsp

— SteamDB (@SteamDB) June 3, 2023

Another useful feature Valve seems to be working on for Steam is totally new. As reported by Ars Technica earlier this month, dataminers found lines of code that seem to reference some kind of “framerate estimator” feature that isn’t currently available. Dataminers also found text that states: “Select an App and a PC config to get a chart of estimated framerates, based on the framerates of other Steam users.” So it seems Valve will leverage other players’ experiences with a game and their hardware configuration to help provide FPS estimations to other players with similar rigs.

This is a great idea and expands on Valve’s (flawed) Verified program for Steam Deck and the upcoming Steam Machine. Being uncertain that a game can run decently on your PC can be a big barrier for players who might not be as PC-savvy as others, so offering customers more information and guidance on possible performance before they buy a game is a smart way to help bring more people into the Steam/PC gaming ecosystem.

Now the question is, when will these features turn up in a future Steam update?



https://kotaku.com/new-steam-feature-could-make-it-easier-to-avoid-getting-ripped-off-2000687943

item.title: Pokémon Go Fans Think They May Have Figured Out Something Niantic Has Kept Hidden For Years
item.content:encoded:
Pokémon Go has finally introduced Silicobra, a monster found primarily in desert areas in the main games. When developer Niantic announced that the snake creature was finally coming to the monster collecting app, it said players would need to seek it out in “desert-like areas.” That’s not exactly specific, so players have had to go outside and test their local geography to see if Pokémon Go would register dry, sandy areas as desert-like. Well, fans now think they may have figured out how Niantic determines which in-game biome a given real-world area will correspond to, and it’s through a United States government website.

Fans on the popular Go subreddit The Silph Road conducted a case study in Christchurch, New Zealand testing if Silicobra would spawn in areas labeled as “Land Cover: Sparsely or Non-vegetated” in the United States Geological Survey. Early results seemed promising, so the group also cross referenced the USGS’ data for other biome-specific Pokémon spawns like Toedscool, which seems to primarily spawn in areas marked as “Grassland,” “Shrubland,” and “Forest.” One Pokémon whose spawn areas the group wasn’t able to verify is Wiglett, which spawns in coastal/marine biomes, which USGS doesn’t track.

For the moment this is just a plausible theory, as it’s not been thoroughly confirmed, but if players have cracked the code on biome-based spawns, they might have found a valuable resource for helping players track down specific Pokémon, and it’s the sort of thing some players feel Niantic should have provided long ago.

“I love Pokémon for the underlying numbers and math that goes into it,” Redditor rachycarebear wrote. “I love developing and testing theories to figure out how and why they might happen, but that should be limited to an optional and highly technical style of gameplay. The basics of ‘find cute creature and catch it’ shouldn’t require this level of research. There needs to be enough easily available information that if I want to catch the Pokémon, I can. Tell me Wigletts are near bodies of water or Toedscools are in parks and I can do that with a basic familiarity of my local geography. Then sure, I’ll enjoy examining which resources are being utilized how, figuring out which areas will have the best hunting, and strategizing to optimize game play and maximize spawns. But that’s because I enjoy that stuff, not because it’s the only way to get the new release.”

Silicobra is part of a “Sustainability Week” event that is running from April 14 to 20. Despite being an event Pokémon, the snake seems to be a rare spawn even in confirmed desert areas, though some players are reporting it showing up in other areas like parks and nearby rivers.



https://kotaku.com/pokemon-go-silicobra-desert-biome-map-usgs-reddit-2000687950

item.title: Insomniac Reaffirms More Wolverine News Will Come in Spring
item.content:encoded:
With less than six months to go before the scheduled release of Wolverine on the PlayStation 5, Insomniac Games has reaffirmed that more news about the game will be shared soon.

The much-anticipated Wolverine game from Insomniac is due to be released in September. The company has previously stated that they want to show more gameplay footage before allowing pre-orders to begin. Still, they regularly encourage players to wishlist the title now. Now, there’s another reminder about impending news.

Insomniac Games once again shared that news about Wolverine will be shared in Spring. Taking to Twitter with a quick message reminding players to wishlist the game, their company stated that more news is coming this spring.

“More news coming later this Spring! Wishlist now,” the message said.

Spring began on March 20, and the first day of summer is set for June 21. We’re currently smack dab in the middle of April.

It is currently unknown what type of presentation will occur when more Wolverine news is shared. There could be a dedicated presentation specifically for that game. However, it could also become a major part of a larger Sony presentation. There was previously a rumor of a Sony PlayStation State of Play presentation on April 16, but no formal announcement of such a presentation ever came. Learn more about that rumor at this link.

In any case, Insider Gaming will keep players informed of when the next set of information regarding Wolverine will be released. We will also share news about when pre-orders will be available.

Are you excited for Wolverine? What are you hoping to see in the game that hasn’t been announced yet? Sound off in our official Discord server. Also, don’t forget to sign up for our weekly newsletter.

item.title: Blizzard Cinematics Team Helping with Fable’s Development
item.content:encoded:
Matt Booty discusses the importance of synergy in developing Xbox titles.

Booty was recently appointed as Microsoft Gaming’s Chief Content Officer. This promotion coincided with Asha Sharma’s appointment as CEO of Microsoft Gaming, following Phil Spencer’s retirement and Sarah Bond’s resignation.

Fable Benefits From Xbox Studio Synergy

Matt Booty recently appeared on the official Xbox podcast, where, while discussing the projects he’s seen from Xbox Studios that inspire him and the different teams under the Xbox banner, he detailed the teams working together. For example, he says the cinematics team of Blizzard is helping with the development of the upcoming Fable title.

“We’ve got the Blizzard cinematics team helping out on Fable. We’ve got our studio in Montreal, Compulsion Games, using the Activision MoCap studio. We’ve got the team at Rare with a lot of multiplayer experience working on Sea of Thieves, helping out Double Fine on Kiln, the pottery game.”

Other examples he provides of this synergy include some of the technology used in State of Decay 2 being utilized to help build Grounded. Now, the experience from Grounded that Obsidian gathered is being used to help develop State of Decay 3.

More on Fable’s Upcoming Release

Fable is slated for release in Autumn 2026. Recently, it was rumored that there were internal discussions about delaying the title. The team behind the game responded to this by letting the world know it was still on track for the Autumn 2026 release.

The overall belief is that they are concerned about the impending release of Grand Theft Auto 6. Learn more about all of that here.

For more on Fable, check out the explanation of why there is no dog companion in the upcoming game.

How do you feel about the synergistic flow of Microsoft’s development teams for upcoming titles? Sound off in our official Discord server. Also, don’t forget to sign up for our weekly newsletter.

item.title: Broken Statikk Shiv Rework Can’t Make It Into League of Legends
item.content:encoded:
Riot Games has reworked Statikk Shiv in League of Legends, and the community is begging for the item change to not make it out of the PBE.

The League of Legends item has been updated by Riot so that now its signature lightning bolts, which strike up to four nearby enemies, will apply all on-hit effects.

Riot promised there would be no big mid-season LoL patch this year so that players don’t get alienated from struggling to keep up.

Yet, this change is essentially game-breaking, with a lot of new combos and builds enabled by this change with a lot of on-hit abilities in the game.

Many of these interactions appear to be much too strong and will likely need to be addressed by Riot should the change still make it from the PBE to the live server.

Statikk Shiv Changes

Statikk Shiv is no longer an ADC item. While still offering 45 AD, 30% attack speed, and 4% movement speed, the new Shiv will also offer 45 AP too, allowing a lot more champions to purchase it.

Because of this, Statikk Shiv has now increased in price from 2700 gold to 3000 gold with a new build path replacing Rectrix with the Aether Wisp AP item.

And of course, the passive ability of the item has been reworked so that the bouncing lightning now applies to all nearby enemies, including minions, which can lead to some pretty crazy interactions.

Broken Statikk Shiv Combos

There are various broken interactions that players have already discovered on the PBE.

For example, while a damage item might not usually be what you want on Braum, the ability to apply his passive to multiple enemies in a teamfight and have all of them stunned could be game-winning.

Similarly, Senna with Shiv and Runaan’s can get up to five stacks from a single auto-attack and Q — allowing her to scale much faster.

There are a lot of other strong item combinations with the new Shiv, including Dusk and Dawn, which has proven to be one of the strongest items in the game this year.

Dusk and Dawn and Shiv will likely be a must on Kai’Sa, who will benefit massively from the dual damage and the ability to mark all of her opponents.

Another strong item combo is Heartsteel, which allies the on-hit to everyone nearby and can also lead to ridiculously fast stacking.

And another melee champ benefiting is Volibear, who can fully stack his passive with just one auto for crazy damage.

And perhaps the most egregious of all is Katarina, who procs Statikk non-stop with her ultimate.

Do you think this item is as broken as it looks? Let us know on the Insider Gaming Discord.

And see what changes have made it live into the game with LoL Patch 26.8.

item.title: Valorborn, A Medieval Fantasy Sandbox RPG, Has Launched Into Early Access On PC Today
item.content:encoded:
It's the week of sandbox RPGs, as Valorborn launches into early access today. The Medieval Fantasy-inspired sandbox aims to bring a player-driven world to players. 

Developed by Turkish studio Laps Games, the sandbox RPG takes place in the kingdom of Thareon in the wake of a "great magical cataclysm." In the world of Valorborn, players can apparently take any role that tickles their fancy, whether it's a sellsword or a tradesman dominating the kingdom's trade routes. It looks like the aim here is to create a compelling player-driven beyond your standard sandbox experience. 

From today's press release:

"In Valorborn, the life you lead is yours to define. Become a sellsword and earn coin by solving the troubles of villagers, live off the wilderness with allies in your own encampment, produce and trade goods, hunt, fight, build, or forge a path entirely your own. There are no fixed classes. Character growth is shaped through the actions players take and the choices they make."

In the future, the plan is apparently to bring more systems, kingdoms, factions and more to Valorborn as it rides into 1.0 down the road. The basic systems are in place during this early access launch, including its progression system, exploration, trade and more. It's a survival game, so you'll be able to create your own base and encampment should you choose with you and some friends. 

You can grab Valorborn on Steam or the Epic Games Store for $24.99 starting today. The team is planning to release a roadmap and workshop support soon, and the team is planning a livestreamed Q&A next week to talk about feedback and more in the wake of today's launch.



https://www.mmorpg.com/news/valorborn-a-medieval-fantasy-sandbox-rpg-has-launched-into-early-access-on-pc-today-2000137811
